name: Strawberry-Rhubarb Jelly
recipeInstructions:
Yield: About 7 half-pints
Procedure:
1. Wash and cut rhubarb into 1-inch pieces.
2. Blend or grind the rhubarb.
3. Wash, stem, and crush strawberries in a saucepan.
4. Place both fruits in a jelly bag or double layer of cheesecloth.
5. Gently squeeze out juice, measuring 3-1/2 cups.
6. Add butter and sugar to the juice, stirring until well mixed.
7. Bring mixture to a boil over high heat, stirring constantly.
8. Stir in pectin and bring to a full rolling boil, boiling hard for 1 minute.
9. Remove from heat, skim off foam, and fill sterile jars, leaving 1/4-inch headspace.
10. Adjust lids and process as recommended in Table 1.
Table 1: Recommended processing time for Strawberry-Rhubarb Jelly in a boiling-water canner.
Style of Pack: Hot
Jar Size: Half-pints or Pints
Process Time at Altitudes:
0 - 1,000 ft: 5 minutes
1,001 - 6,000 ft: 10 minutes
Above 6,000 ft: 15 minutes
